title: Being & greeting: ვარ, გამარჯობა
focus: Present of 'to be', pronouns, hello/thank-you register.

title: Having things & people: მაქვს / მყავს
focus: The animate/inanimate 'have' split — things vs living beings.

title: Wanting & knowing: მინდა, ვიცი
focus: Core desire/knowledge verbs; first taste of dative.

title: Questions, numbers, prices
focus: რა/ვინ/სად/როდის/როგორ/რატომ/რამდენი + counting + money.

title: The ვ- team: present conjugation
focus: Subject markers ვ-/-/-ს and plural -თ/-ენ, explicitly drilled.

title: Daily routine verbs
focus: More present verbs; talking through your day.

title: Object markers: მ-, გ-, გვ-
focus: Who it happens TO: გელოდები, მეხმარები, გირეკავ.

title: Where things are: -ში, -ზე
focus: Location and direction glued onto the noun.

title: Whose & with whom: -ის, -თან
focus: Genitive possession and the -თან postposition.

title: Checkpoint: shopping & money
focus: Consolidate weeks 1-9 at the bazaar.

title: The future: preverb + present
focus: გა-ვაკეთებ, ვ-ი-ყიდი — preverbs unlock the future.

title: Plans & promises
focus: Future practice, წავალ/მოვალ.

title: მი- vs მო-: away or toward me
focus: Preverbs as vocabulary: direction relative to the speaker.

title: The past + the subject's costume (-მა)
focus: Aorist arrives — and with it, the -მა subject on doing-verbs.

title: Past of your daily verbs
focus: Yesterday, told fluently.

title: Past questions & negatives
focus: ვერ vs არ, asking what happened.

title: Irregular pasts
focus: წავედი, ვთქვი, ვნახე, მივეცი — memorize the rebels.

title: Verb-nouns: ცურვა მიყვარს
focus: Masdars as everyday vocabulary.

title: შენ vs თქვენ: reading the room
focus: Politeness switching, in-law mode.

title: Checkpoint: tell a story
focus: Narrate a simple past story end to end.

title: The flipped past: მინახავს
focus: 'Have done' — the construction that turns the sentence inside out.

title: Have you ever…?
focus: გინახავს? გიჭამია? Experience questions.

title: The to-me family: მიყვარს & friends
focus: Feeling verbs with dative experiencers.

title: Would & could
focus: Conditional -დი forms for wishes and politeness.

title: Should & must: უნდა
focus: უნდა + subjunctive for advice and obligation.

title: Connectors & flow
focus: რომ, თუ, როცა, იმიტომ რომ — glue for real speech.

title: Health & the pharmacy
focus: მტკივა-complaints, buying medicine, describing symptoms.

title: Opinions & polite disagreement
focus: ვფიქრობ რომ…, მართალი ხარ, არ გეთანხმები.

title: Storytelling
focus: Mixing past forms in longer narration.

title: The supra
focus: Toasts, გაუმარჯოს, holding your own at the table.
